A huge museum with plenty of exhibits. Plan to stay at least two hours. Many iconic cars are on display including many from the Fast & Furious films. This is a must see if you;re in LA and walking distance from the La Brea Tar Pits. Best of all, there is an amazing restaurant on site! It's a little pricey but the food was excellent!

Visited on a weekday morning and spent about 2.5 hours here, which felt like the right amount of time for a relaxed visit. The building itself is impressive, and there are plenty of interesting cars even if you're not a huge automotive fan. I used public transit to get there and found it pretty straightforward. If you're driving, just keep in mind that parking isn't free. I arrived shortly after opening and walked straight in without any line. One thing that surprised me was how much time I spent moving between the different floors and exhibits, so expect to be on your feet more than you might think. Going early definitely made the experience more enjoyable since it stayed relatively quiet for most of my visit.

For the car enthusiast or anyone interested in the development of transportation with vehicles, this is the right place. 
Over several floors, almost everything related to vehicles is on display. Additionally, you can find here, among other things, a version of the DeLorean DMC-12 from the "Back to the Future" series, as well as a version of the "Batmobile." You can also find exhibits from the early days of mobility to future visions and the currently emerging self-driving vehicles (Waymo Robotaxi, Zoox Taxi) - well done and laid out - definitely worth a visit!
